description: Language-neutral knowledge base of BitBox02 firmware constraints. Consumed by /go/bitbox/quirks and /ts/src/quirks alike. See SCHEMA.md for field semantics.

quirks.name: non-ascii-eip712-string
quirks.description: Firmware rejects EIP-712 string values containing non-ASCII bytes (umlauts, emojis) with ErrInvalidInput 101. Client must transliterate (e.g. NFKD + ASCII fallback) before signTypedData.
quirks.source: Observed in production; ErrInvalidInput=101 in api/firmware/error.go

detect.reason: non-ASCII string literal in a file that touches EIP-712 / signTyped APIs
detect.fix_hint: transliterate via NFKD + ASCII fallback before sending to BitBox firmware

quirks.name: eth-nonce-max-16-bytes
quirks.description: nonce field of ETHSignRequest/EIP1559Request is capped at 16 bytes (smallest big-endian encoding). Leading zero-bytes or values > 2^128 are rejected.
quirks.source: messages/eth.proto: nonce documented as max 16 bytes

quirks.name: eth-recipient-must-be-20-bytes
quirks.description: recipient field of ETHSignRequest must be exactly 20 bytes. Anything else (EIP-55 strings, 0x-prefixed hex, ENS resolved late) is rejected with ErrInvalidInput.

quirks.name: eth-value-max-32-bytes
quirks.description: value field is capped at 32 bytes (smallest big-endian). Values > 2^256 are firmware-rejected.

quirks.name: eth-eip1559-fee-fields-max-16-bytes
quirks.description: max_priority_fee_per_gas and max_fee_per_gas are each capped at 16 bytes (smallest big-endian).

quirks.name: eth-numerics-must-be-minimal-bigendian
quirks.description: All ETH numeric fields require 'smallest big-endian serialization'. Leading zero bytes (incl. left-pad to 32) cause ErrInvalidInput. Strip leading zeros before sending.
quirks.source: messages/eth.proto: documented for all numeric fields

quirks.name: eth-address-case-enum-bounded
quirks.description: address_case enum accepts 0 (MIXED), 1 (UPPER), 2 (LOWER) only. Other values rejected.

quirks.name: eth-data-length-must-match-streamed-data
quirks.description: When using streamed data signing, data_length must exactly match the byte count actually streamed. Mismatch results in firmware abort mid-stream.

quirks.name: eth-empty-data-zero-value-requires-v9.15.0+
quirks.description: Firmware < v9.15.0 rejects ETH transactions with empty data AND zero value (no-op transfers). v9.15.0 allows them.
quirks.source: CHANGELOG v9.15.0: 'Allow ETH transactions with empty data + zero value'

quirks.name: eth-known-network-list-versioned
quirks.description: Firmware ships an allowlist of well-known chain IDs. New chains (HYPE, SONIC added v9.23.1) are unknown on older firmware and need explicit user-confirmation flows or 'unknown network' UX.

quirks.name: btc-locktime-below-500m
quirks.description: BTCSignInitRequest.locktime must be < 500_000_000. Timestamp-style locktimes are firmware-rejected.

quirks.name: btc-version-must-be-1-or-2
quirks.description: BTCSignInitRequest.version must be 1 or 2. Other transaction versions are rejected.

quirks.name: btc-sequence-restricted-values
quirks.description: BTCSignInputRequest.sequence must be 0xffffffff, 0xffffffff-1 (RBF), or 0xffffffff-2. Pre-v9.16.0 emitted warning for other values.

quirks.name: btc-payload-size-matches-address-type
quirks.description: BTCSignOutputRequest.payload must be 20 bytes for p2pkh/p2sh/p2wpkh and 32 bytes for p2wsh. Mismatch triggers firmware reject.

quirks.name: btc-signature-lengths-format-specific
quirks.description: Transaction signatures: 64 bytes (32R+32S big-endian). Message signatures: 65 bytes (64 + 1 recovery ID). Mismatched length on client fails verification.

quirks.name: btc-multisig-account-limit-25
quirks.description: Firmware caps registered multisig accounts at 25 (was 10 pre-v9.6.0). Attempts beyond the limit are rejected.

quirks.name: btc-fee-attack-prev-out-index-check
quirks.description: From v9.15.0 firmware validates the index of each input's previous output against the supplied prevout (anti-fee-attack). Older firmware accepted inconsistent indexes — silent fee-inflation vector.
quirks.source: CHANGELOG v9.15.0: 'check index of input's previous output to prevent the fee attack'

quirks.name: cardano-max-tx-16kb
quirks.description: Cardano transactions are capped at 16384 bytes (CIP-0009). Large token bundles or many UTXO inputs may exceed.

quirks.name: cardano-network-mainnet-testnet-only
quirks.description: network enum accepts CardanoMainnet (0) or CardanoTestnet (1). Other values are rejected.

quirks.name: cardano-drep-type-enum-bounded
quirks.description: Vote-delegation DRep type accepts KEY_HASH, SCRIPT_HASH, ALWAYS_ABSTAIN, ALWAYS_NO_CONFIDENCE only. Other values are rejected.

quirks.name: cardano-duplicate-token-keys-rejected
quirks.description: From v9.9.1, firmware rejects Cardano outputs containing duplicate token keys (same policy_id + asset_name in one output). Pre-v9.9.1 quietly merged — undefined value behaviour.
quirks.source: CHANGELOG v9.9.1: 'Disallow duplicate token keys in Cardano outputs'

quirks.name: mnemonic-18-words-removed
quirks.description: From v9.24.0 firmware no longer accepts 18-word recovery phrases — only 12 or 24. Clients exposing an 18-word UI flow fail on newer firmware.
quirks.source: CHANGELOG v9.24.0: 'Removed support for 18-word recovery phrases'

detect.reason: code or UI exposes an 18-word recovery option; v9.24.0+ firmware rejects 18-word phrases
detect.fix_hint: drop the 18-word option from any picker; offer only 12 or 24

quirks.name: mnemonic-final-word-checksum-restricted
quirks.description: When restoring 12 or 18 (pre-v9.24.0) words, firmware restricts the final word to checksum-valid candidates. Clients must surface only valid candidates, not the full BIP-39 list.

quirks.name: mnemonic-zero-seed-words-versioned
quirks.description: Pre-v9.9.0 firmware rejected recovery phrases deriving a zero seed. v9.9.0 accepts them. Dev test-seeds may behave inconsistently across firmware versions.
quirks.source: CHANGELOG v9.9.0: 'allow recovery words that convert to a zero seed'

quirks.name: pairing-channel-hash-before-confirm
quirks.description: bitbox02-api-go's pair() call blocks waiting for on-device confirmation. Channel hash is available BEFORE confirm. Sequential clients display nothing until user has confirmed. Workaround: parallel-poll the channel hash while pair() blocks.

quirks.name: ble-init-frame-retransmit-must-pass
quirks.description: BLE links legitimately retransmit init frames. Plugin-side de-dup logic must NOT drop them — otherwise multi-page signing aborts mid-flow (e.g. 1/13 → 2/13). Historical bug: seenPackets.removeAll() before seenPackets.contains() check.

detect.reason: seenPackets.clear/removeAll/delete appears before contains/has/includes — legitimate retransmits get dropped
detect.fix_hint: reorder so the membership check runs before any removal

quirks.name: btc-sequence-warning-changed-9.16.0
quirks.description: Pre-v9.16.0 firmware emitted a warning for 'unusual' sequence numbers in BTC inputs. v9.16.0 removed it. Clients building cross-version UX must handle both shapes.

quirks.name: gomobile-export-must-recover-panic
quirks.description: Every gomobile-exported function called from the host app must defer-recover, otherwise a Go panic crashes the host. (Analogous: TS WebView bridges must wrap WASM-side throws so the RN side gets a rejected promise rather than a silent stall.)

quirks.name: transport-timeout-must-be-context-driven
quirks.description: Hard-coded transport timeouts (e.g. 10s) block user-confirm flows where the user takes longer than the timeout. Use context deadlines with confirmation-aware extensions.

detect.reason: hard-coded 10-second timeout in transport code blocks long user-confirm flows
detect.fix_hint: switch to context-driven deadlines that can be extended during long user-confirm flows

quirks.name: antiklepto-host-nonce-commitment-required
quirks.description: Modern firmware requires a host-nonce commitment phase before signing as anti-klepto protection. Clients skipping this fall back to unprotected signing — exposes key-recovery via biased nonces.
